beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Aiden is a night owl but has early condo showings; yesterday's low energy (35/100) means a brisk, efficient morning rather than leisurely. Coordinates -122.207118, 47.676563 resolve to Kirkland.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila office; transit strike means no Light Rail, so car is only option.
beats.reasoning: Citywide transit strike until 4pm forces Aiden to drive despite surge pricing; Kirkland to Tukwila is ~45 min in normal traffic, but strike + Thursday morning congestion warrants a full hour.

beats.activity: Listing prep, client calls, and condo showing coordination at office.
beats.reasoning: Routine morning work block; Aiden handles administrative tasks and preps for downtown showings later in the day.

beats.activity: Lunch from food truck pod while sitting in car, scrolling emails.
beats.reasoning: Aiden knows every food truck's rotation and often eats this way; quick, efficient meal while staying at the office complex.

beats.activity: Drive downtown for condo showings; transit strike means rideshare surge pricing but unavoidable.
beats.reasoning: Tukwila to Downtown is ~35–45 min; transit strike adds pressure but Aiden is a working agent—showings must happen. Light traffic mid-day helps.

beats.activity: Back-to-back condo showings in downtown units; partly cloudy weather is nice for viewing light and outdoor amenities.
beats.reasoning: Two or three showings, ~45 min each with walk-throughs and client questions. Partly cloudy (68F) is ideal showing weather—not too hot, good natural light.

beats.activity: Brief stop at Pike Place Market for snack and fresh air; recharge before heading home.
beats.reasoning: Aiden values decompression; a quick 20–30 min walk through the market is a mood reset after showings, and it's on the way before heading home.

beats.activity: Drive back to Kirkland; transit strike still in effect until 4pm (now past), but prefer car anyway.
beats.reasoning: Downtown to Kirkland ~45 min; late afternoon drive is manageable. Aiden uses the time to decompress and mentally close the workday.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and meal at home; simple, nourishing after a long day.
beats.reasoning: Low energy yesterday (35/100) carries into today; Aiden opts for a quiet home dinner rather than going out. Partly cloudy weather doesn't push him toward outdoor dining.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: check listings online, catch up on client emails, light reading.
beats.reasoning: Aiden is a night owl; evening work-adjacent tasks feel natural. No salsa dancing or kayaking tonight—energy is moderate, and transit strike made the day logistically demanding.

beats.activity: Wind down: shower, prepare for bed, light stretching to ease shoulder tension.
beats.reasoning: Aiden tends to loosen shoulders after work; a gentle routine helps transition to sleep. Ends well before midnight to allow the overnight sleep beat.
7-hour sleep block; Aiden is a night owl but needs recovery after a transit-strike day. Sleep ends at 6am, crossing into Friday as required.

diary: Started the day with my usual routine—shower, coffee, the whole thing. The transit strike meant I had to drive to Tukwila instead of taking the Light Rail, which honestly wasn't the worst thing. Got some prep work done at the office, fielded a few calls. Grabbed lunch from the food truck pod while catching up on emails in the car. The afternoon was the real grind: back-to-back showings downtown. The partly cloudy weather actually worked in my favor—those units looked sharp with the natural light coming through. I needed a break after all that, so I swung by Pike Place Market for a few minutes. Just stood there with a coffee, watching the crowds. Felt like I could breathe again. Drove home as the transit strike was winding down. Made a simple dinner, checked listings, tried to unwind with some stretching. My shoulders are killing me.
thought_process: The strike threw a wrench in things, but honestly it forced me to stick with the car, which gave me more control over my schedule. I could've stressed about the rideshare surge pricing, but I was already driving anyway. The showings downtown felt productive—the weather was on our side, and I had two serious prospects. I think the Pike Place stop was necessary; I was running on fumes and needed that reset, even if it was just ten minutes. By evening, I was tired but satisfied. The day felt purposeful, not scattered. I'm noticing my body's asking for more recovery time though—the shoulder thing is getting worse.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
